title: CRM email tracking across different leads with one customer

posts.author: cvanurk (Christian van Urk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday community,

Recently my company and I have implemented a system for email tracking. We do this with the Emails & Activities app integrated with our personal Outlook email accounts.

Leads are added as seperate items into our CRM. To these leads we assign a contact person that represents our client. These contact details are stored in a different board in our CRM. However, we work with clients that have a number of seperate leads & projects assigned to them. This means that ‘lead x’, ‘lead y’ and ‘lead z’ can all have ‘person x’ assigned to them as our contact person, yet these leads are seperate unrelated projects.

We now run into the issue that when ‘person x’ sends us an email related to lead x, his email is tracked in the Emails & Actitivies app ‘lead y’ and ‘lead z’ as well, and vice versa. This gets very confusion and a mix up of information.

How can we prevent this? I am looking for a solution to track emailing activity, but specifically across different leads and projects with one client.

posts.author: Jake_Gro (Jake Grossman)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a huge issue with emails being accessed by others under the same license. Others are able to see my emails from other workspaces and when boards are private and when I don’t even enable the Emails & Activities. I have an automation that adds the emails from a column as an update. However, anyone in our institution’s account can access my email updates if they have the Emails & Activities enabled. There is no real fix for this at this time, even with so many Monday users having issues and submitting tickets.

posts.author: Hirof (Yolly)
posts.cookedHtml: I stopped email tracking after the million of confusing leads created all over my CRM. Indeed, monday.com hasn’t solved this issue yet, but turning CRM into a complete mess isn’t a solution at all.

posts.author: mactoph (Christopher Phillips)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a huge issue for us as well - here is a feature request I created describing how things work with some changes we would like to see: Board specific Emails & Activities settings

Hopefully this is something on their road map!

posts.author: Hirof (Yolly)
posts.cookedHtml: Lead enrichment should be done in the most accurate way without duplicates or missing data. Thus, the overarching goal of lead enrichment to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of lead generation by marketing and sales teams will be easier achieved.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone! Just wanted to let you know that tomorrow at 11am EST we are having an event all about our sales CRM hosted by monday.com sales CRM lead product manager! You will learn all about why this product was created, how you can use it, and get your questions answered! You don’t want to miss this. Register here!

posts.author: GabbyG (Gabby)
posts.cookedHtml: I wonder if there is a solution to this issue, now in 2025?

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Gabby,

Not sure if this is solved in E&A already. But there’s a marketplace app called SuperMail that has similar functionality to emails and activities, but is documents the emails per item ID, rather than per email column so all conversations are documented properly per the specific lead/project/ticket.

auth.monday.com

SuperMail – Email Automation - monday.com Marketplace

Emails designed, sent, and tracked inside monday.com

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a really common limitation of Emails & Activities - it tracks by email address, not by context (lead/project), so once the same contact email is reused, everything gets mixed together.

There isn’t a native way today to scope emails to only one item when the same email exists on multiple items. A practical workaround we’ve seen work is separating email capture from email tracking:

For example, some teams use Email to Tasks for monday.com to forward or BCC project-specific emails into the correct lead or project item. That way, the email is attached only where it’s relevant, instead of auto-logging everywhere the contact appears.

This keeps communication clean per lead/project while still preserving history. Not perfect, but until monday supports per-item email scoping, this approach avoids cross-polluted timelines.
